evidence_contract.question: Can we define a shared target for CUT4 and Zhao--Cui that preserves the generalized SV likelihood rather than only a transformed-residual diagnostic?
evidence_contract.baseline_or_comparator: The target-definition table is the governing artifact. Tiny CUT4 transformed-residual and moment-matched Kalman checks are diagnostic only. No matched generalized-SV Zhao--Cui equality target exists in this phase.
evidence_contract.primary_promotion_criterion: The target-definition table fills all required fields; exact native, transformed-residual, Gaussian-mixture, and moment-matched routes are distinguished; the decision remains P42 Class D diagnostic only; finite diagnostics stay within dims<=3, T<=2, CPU-only, and CUT4 augmented dimension<=6; no same-target value/gradient equality test is run.
veto_diagnostics: transformed residual described as exact native likelihood
Gaussian mixture approximation described as exact
s_t and h_t independence assumptions used after observation coupling without disclosure
same-target generalized-SV value/gradient equality row run before a reviewed native/reference route exists

commands.purpose: Run focused P44-M7 generalized-SV target-definition and diagnostic-only evidence.
Compile highdim package and the focused M7 test file.

diagnostics.summary: Local M7 evidence completes the target table and preserves generalized SV as P42 Class D diagnostic only. Focused pytest reports 4 passed. CUT4 transformed-residual diagnostic value is -3.560288e+00 and score is -2.456627e+00. Moment-matched Kalman is explicitly non-exact, and the scalar Zhao--Cui route rejects the two-state generalized-SV diagnostic model.

long_run_controls.resource_caps: No long run; target table plus tiny CPU-only diagnostic checks only.
